page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at 2.5 GHz. It features 16 physical cores, 16 vCPUs, and 128.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance utilizes the KVM hypervisor and includes 35 MB of L3 cache, along with support for AVX and AVX512F instruction sets. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core workloads. The instance exhibits weak single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by its low Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core scores, both landing in the bottom 10% to 25% categories. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores all placing in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth remains average for both small and large blocks, though the Passmark memory score is weak.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, the ecs.se1ne.4xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require high single-threaded CPU performance. It is well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded text or image processing. It can also handle light, small-scale LLM inference tasks, though performance drops significantly on larger models. It is not recommended for single-threaded compute-intensive tasks or workloads requiring local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.4xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications that leverage parallel processing. It is best suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads, while being less optimal for single-threaded or storage-heavy tasks.
